**Session 2022-23**

chunks.text:
Section heading: Online Safety Bill

**Written evidence submitted by Dave 'Yardfish' (OSB93)**

**Online Safety Bill**

I am a YouTube channel owner and Independent Investigator who monitors the Internet for violent and harmful content relating to the torture of animals, specifically monkeys. I am aware of the UK Online Safety Bill and am writing to draw your attention to this issue as it relates to the Bill.

**Background**

Over the past two years I have been archiving and documenting the growing trend of "Baby Monkey Torture", which has become prolific on both Youtube and Facebook.

The ‘Baby Monkey Torture’ community began with a small group of individuals who fetishize the suffering and misery of infant monkeys, and who create videos and playlists of content that appeal to their tastes, sometimes making use of lab footage and wildlife channel clips to string together compilations of animals being subjected to some form of violence or trauma. In some instances, the content owners purport to be "rescue" or ‘pet" channels, but place animals in deliberately contrived, dangerous, and sometimes fatal situations for the purpose of increasing views and revenue, as outlined by the Social Media Animal Cruelty Report.(1)
chunks.display_text:
Section heading: Online Safety Bill

**Written evidence submitted by Dave 'Yardfish' (OSB93)**

**Online Safety Bill**

I am a YouTube channel owner and Independent Investigator who monitors the Internet for violent and harmful content relating to the torture of animals, specifically monkeys. I am aware of the UK Online Safety Bill and am writing to draw your attention to this issue as it relates to the Bill.

**Background**

Over the past two years I have been archiving and documenting the growing trend of "Baby Monkey Torture", which has become prolific on both Youtube and Facebook.

The ‘Baby Monkey Torture’ community began with a small group of individuals who fetishize the suffering and misery of infant monkeys, and who create videos and playlists of content that appeal to their tastes, sometimes making use of lab footage and wildlife channel clips to string together compilations of animals being subjected to some form of violence or trauma. In some instances, the content owners purport to be "rescue" or ‘pet" channels, but place animals in deliberately contrived, dangerous, and sometimes fatal situations for the purpose of increasing views and revenue, as outlined by the Social Media Animal Cruelty Report.(1)

chunks.text: being subjected to some form of violence or trauma. In some instances, the content owners purport to be "rescue" or ‘pet" channels, but place animals in deliberately contrived, dangerous, and sometimes fatal situations for the purpose of increasing views and revenue, as outlined by the Social Media Animal Cruelty Report.(1) Over time, the scope and scale of violence and abuse being depicted has dramatically escalated. Some members of this community have brokered deals with owners of other channels to provide custom content depicting specific types of torture, often referred to as "discipline" and primarily feature acts of sexual sadism and genital mutilation. This operation was exposed by the NGOs ‘Lady FreeThinker’ and "Action For Primates’ in 2021.(2) In just over a year, what began as a small community of individuals has grown to a commercial venture that has gone unchecked and unregulated by platforms such as YouTube. As time goes by, these communities continue to grow and the content they produce and distribute becomes more and more extreme. **Animal torture videos as it relates to the UK Online Safety Bill** Proliferation of Content ⦁ In the absence of any form of consequence or deterrence by platforms used to host content, this fetish community has rapidly escalated in the degree of sadism and violence depicted. In less than one year content has escalated from videos of baby monkeys being teased and hit to commissioned, fee-based videos of (for example,) an infant monkey having its eyes drilled out by a power tool or newborn females being molested with a heated soldering iron.
chunks.display_text: Over time, the scope and scale of violence and abuse being depicted has dramatically escalated. Some members of this community have brokered deals with owners of other channels to provide custom content depicting specific types of torture, often referred to as "discipline" and primarily feature acts of sexual sadism and genital mutilation. This operation was exposed by the NGOs ‘Lady FreeThinker’ and "Action For Primates’ in 2021.(2) In just over a year, what began as a small community of individuals has grown to a commercial venture that has gone unchecked and unregulated by platforms such as YouTube. As time goes by, these communities continue to grow and the content they produce and distribute becomes more and more extreme. **Animal torture videos as it relates to the UK Online Safety Bill** Proliferation of Content ⦁ In the absence of any form of consequence or deterrence by platforms used to host content, this fetish community has rapidly escalated in the degree of sadism and violence depicted. In less than one year content has escalated from videos of baby monkeys being teased and hit to commissioned, fee-based videos of (for example,) an infant monkey having its eyes drilled out by a power tool or newborn females being molested with a heated soldering iron.

chunks.text: sadism and violence depicted. In less than one year content has escalated from videos of baby monkeys being teased and hit to commissioned, fee-based videos of (for example,) an infant monkey having its eyes drilled out by a power tool or newborn females being molested with a heated soldering iron. ⦁ This absence of deterrence has also allowed this community to balloon in size over the last year, moving from a handful of individuals, to hundreds of producers and distributors who make open use of their chosen platforms to distribute and advertise content. This creates and perpetuates an environment that normalizes violence by providing positive reinforcement and a safe space to individuals who enjoy this type of content. ⦁ Many of the channels featuring the abuse of animals on youtube are fully monetized and gain revenue via ads that are played throughout their runtime. More clicks and watchtime correlate to greater earnings, as such the individuals running these channels are further incentivized to create abusive content that consistently draws viewers. ⦁ Once an individual has viewed one or more of these videos, the youtube algorithm then further recommends content of a similar nature at a more and more aggressive pace, the more content is consumed further pushing the viewer 'down the rabbit hole' of animal abuse content online. A simple search for the phrase 'baby monkey abuse' will reveal thousands of videos curated towards this community. Unrestricted access and impact on children ⦁ Violent and harmful content is broadly available for public viewing with little restriction, and often stumbled upon by minors. Since at least 2013 (3), some videos are deliberately targeted to minors, making use of cartoons and music associated with children's entertainment in the first few minutes of their videos before switching to graphic torture content.
chunks.display_text: ⦁ This absence of deterrence has also allowed this community to balloon in size over the last year, moving from a handful of individuals, to hundreds of producers and distributors who make open use of their chosen platforms to distribute and advertise content. This creates and perpetuates an environment that normalizes violence by providing positive reinforcement and a safe space to individuals who enjoy this type of content. ⦁ Many of the channels featuring the abuse of animals on youtube are fully monetized and gain revenue via ads that are played throughout their runtime. More clicks and watchtime correlate to greater earnings, as such the individuals running these channels are further incentivized to create abusive content that consistently draws viewers. ⦁ Once an individual has viewed one or more of these videos, the youtube algorithm then further recommends content of a similar nature at a more and more aggressive pace, the more content is consumed further pushing the viewer 'down the rabbit hole' of animal abuse content online. A simple search for the phrase 'baby monkey abuse' will reveal thousands of videos curated towards this community. Unrestricted access and impact on children ⦁ Violent and harmful content is broadly available for public viewing with little restriction, and often stumbled upon by minors. Since at least 2013 (3), some videos are deliberately targeted to minors, making use of cartoons and music associated with children's entertainment in the first few minutes of their videos before switching to graphic torture content.

chunks.text: broadly available for public viewing with little restriction, and often stumbled upon by minors. Since at least 2013 (3), some videos are deliberately targeted to minors, making use of cartoons and music associated with children's entertainment in the first few minutes of their videos before switching to graphic torture content. ⦁ The ease with which animal abuse content can be created, uploaded, and distributed on social media and engagement-driven platforms lends itself to individuals seeking recognition and attention, many of whom are minors. One example is the YouTube channel "Peluchin Entertainment" run by a minor who abused and tortured a cat online, eventually spawning a slew of copycat channels also run by minors. (4) ⦁ Studies have shown that exposure to animal abuse content can lead to maladaptive psychological behaviors in human children. Exposure in general, to particularly extreme, violent content can provide significant and sometimes traumatic developmental challenges in developing children. (5,6) In summary, the purpose of this submission is to draw attention to the unrestricted access to violent and harmful content involving animal torture, the alarming rate at which it has escalated, and the potential impact on children and vulnerable persons. I ask that further consideration be given to regulating this content as a part of the online safety bill. Sincerely, Dave 'Yardfish' **Reference** 1: https://www.smaccoalition.com/smacc-report 2: https://ladyfreethinker.org/youtube-monkey-haters-form-private-group-where-members-are- paying-to-have-baby-monkeys-tortured-and-killed-on-camera/ 3: https://www.independent.co.uk/tech/children-lured-to-watch-animal-cruelty-online-8598178.html 4: https://www.sportskeeda.com/esports/youtube-bans-alleged-animal-abuser-peluchin- entertainment-internet-rejoices 5: https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0145213417303356?via%3Dihub 6: https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0145213417303356?via%3Dihub *June 2022*

Exposure in general, to particularly extreme, violent content can provide significant and sometimes traumatic developmental challenges in developing children. (5,6) In summary, the purpose of this submission is to draw attention to the unrestricted access to violent and harmful content involving animal torture, the alarming rate at which it has escalated, and the potential impact on children and vulnerable persons. I ask that further consideration be given to regulating this content as a part of the online safety bill.
